 {"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-295","title":"History: Implement data export to JSON and CSV formats","description":"## Summary
Export historical usage data for external analysis and backup.

## Commands
- `caut history export --format json --output history.json`
- `caut history export --format csv --output history.csv`
- `caut history export --format json` (stdout)

## JSON Format
```json
{
  "exported_at": "2026-01-18T12:00:00Z",
  "range": {
    "start": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",
    "end": "2026-01-18T12:00:00Z"
  },
  "snapshots": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "provider": "claude",
      "fetched_at": "2026-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      "primary_used_pct": 45.5,
      "secondary_used_pct": 32.0,
      "tertiary_used_pct": null
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_snapshots": 1234,
    "providers": ["claude", "codex"],
    "date_range_days": 18
  }
}
```

## CSV Format
```csv
id,provider,fetched_at,primary_used_pct,secondary_used_pct,tertiary_used_pct
1,claude,2026-01-15T10:30:00Z,45.5,32.0,
2,codex,2026-01-15T10:30:05Z,28.0,,
```

## Filtering Options
- `--since DATE` - Start date
- `--until DATE` - End date  
- `--provider NAME` - Single provider
- `--limit N` - Max rows

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JSON export with proper structure
- [ ] CSV export with proper escaping
- [ ] Date filtering works
- [ ] Provider filtering works
- [ ] Large exports don't OOM (streaming)
- [ ] Stdout output for piping","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:01:58.830712418Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:01:58.830712418Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-295","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-smv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:03:56.204279143Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-2k1","title":"Unit tests for providers/codex.rs (CRITICAL)","description":"## Overview
🚨 CRITICAL: Provider tests have 0% coverage. This is the highest priority.

## Target: src/providers/codex/mod.rs (581 lines)

## Test Cases - Organized by Function

### 1. JWT Decoding (SECURITY-CRITICAL)
**`decode_jwt_payload()` and `base64_decode()`**
- Valid 3-part JWT decoding
- Invalid JWT format (wrong number of parts)
- Base64url to standard base64 conversion (- to +, _ to /)
- Padding calculation for base64
- Malformed base64 handling
- Invalid JSON in payload
- Real-world OpenAI JWT samples from fixtures

### 2. Auth File Handling
**`read_local_auth()`**
- Valid auth.json with OPENAI_API_KEY
- Valid auth.json with OAuth tokens (id_token, access_token)
- Missing auth.json file
- Empty auth.json
- Malformed JSON

**`is_authenticated()`**
- Returns true with API key only
- Returns true with tokens only
- Returns true with both
- Returns false with neither

**`get_local_identity()`**
- Extract email from JWT claims
- Extract organization from default org in JWT
- Extract subscription info (plan_type, dates)
- Fallback to account_id when JWT decode fails
- API key auth path (no identity info)
- Unauthenticated path

### 3. Fetch Plan Creation
**`fetch_plan()`**
- Returns two strategies (web-dashboard, cli-rpc)
- Web dashboard availability check (platform-conditional)
- CLI availability check via which::which

**`is_cli_available()`**
- Returns true when codex binary in PATH
- Returns false when missing

### 4. CLI Version Parsing
**`get_cli_version()`**
- Parse "codex 0.6.0" format
- Parse "0.6.0" format (version only)
- Handle CLI error output

### 5. Rate Limit Response Parsing
**`try_json_rate_limit()`**
- Test all command variations tried
- Handle command not found errors

**`parse_rate_limit_response()`**
- Full response with all fields
- Response with only rate_limit
- Response with only credits
- Response with only user
- Empty/null fields
- Calculate used_percent from remaining_percent
- Weekly reset calculation (10080 minutes = 7 days)
- Timestamp parsing for resets_at

### 6. Main Fetch Functions
**`fetch_cli()`**
- Successful fetch with identity extraction
- No auth.json exists
- Invalid auth.json
- Integration with get_local_identity

**`fetch_web_dashboard()`**
- Returns UnsupportedSource on non-macOS
- (macOS-only tests if applicable)

**`fetch_credits()`**
- Successful credits fetch
- No credits data available error

## Fixture Requirements
- Sample ~/.codex/auth.json with real JWT structure
- Sample CodexRateLimitResponse JSON
- Real OpenAI JWT tokens (with claims sanitized)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JWT decode/encode round-trip tests pass
- [ ] All auth.json parsing paths covered
- [ ] Identity extraction verified for all auth methods
- [ ] Rate limit parsing tested with real response shapes
- [ ] Error paths tested (missing files, malformed data)
- [ ] Platform-conditional code tested","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T07:14:43.103332900Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T15:56:52.409006145Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T15:56:52.409006145Z","close_reason":"38 tests now passing covering JWT decoding, auth JSON parsing, rate limit responses, organizations, and fixture-based tests","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-2k1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-32d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T07:18:16.762532070Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-2k1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-5b7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T07:18:16.813617693Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-2tn","title":"[EPIC] Credential Health Monitoring and Warnings","description":"## Overview

Credential Health Monitoring proactively warns users before authentication failures occur. Instead of discovering broken auth when something fails, users get advance warning: "Your Claude OAuth token expires in 3 days."

## Strategic Importance (Why P1)

- **Proactive > Reactive**: Prevents frustrating debugging sessions
- **Builds trust**: Tool that warns you feels reliable
- **Low complexity**: Extends existing doctor infrastructure
- **Table stakes**: Expected behavior for any auth-aware tool

## User Value Proposition

**Current state**: User is working, everything seems fine, then suddenly Claude calls fail with auth errors. User spends 30 minutes debugging before realizing token expired.

**Target state**:
```
$ caut doctor
System Health Check
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✓ Claude CLI: installed (v1.2.3)
✓ Codex CLI: installed (v0.6.0)
⚠ Claude OAuth: token expires in 3 days
  → Run: caut auth refresh claude
✓ Codex Auth: valid (expires in 28 days)
✓ Network: all providers reachable
```

Or integrated into regular usage:
```
$ caut usage
⚠️ Heads up: Your Claude token expires in 3 days

Claude: 45% used...
```

## Technical Approach

### JWT Expiration Checking

Most OAuth tokens are JWTs with `exp` claim:
```rust
fn check_token_expiry(token: &str) -> CredentialStatus {
    let claims = decode_jwt_payload(token)?;
    let exp = claims.exp?;
    let now = Utc::now().timestamp();
    let days_until = (exp - now) / 86400;
    
    match days_until {
        d if d < 0 => CredentialStatus::Expired,
        d if d < 7 => CredentialStatus::ExpiringSoon { days: d },
        d => CredentialStatus::Valid { expires_in_days: d },
    }
}
```

### Auth Freshness Tracking

Track last successful authentication per provider:
```rust
struct AuthHealth {
    provider: Provider,
    last_success: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    last_failure: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    consecutive_failures: u32,
    token_status: CredentialStatus,
}
```

### Warning Thresholds

- **7 days**: Informational notice
- **3 days**: Warning with action suggestion
- **1 day**: Urgent warning
- **Expired**: Error with remediation steps

## Integration Points

1. **Doctor command**: Comprehensive credential health check
2. **Usage command**: Header warning if issues detected
3. **Watch mode**: Persistent banner for credential issues
4. **Background check**: Optional periodic validation

## Commands

- `caut doctor` - Full health check (existing, enhanced)
- `caut doctor --credentials` - Credential-focused check
- `caut auth status` - Credential status for all providers
- `caut auth refresh <provider>` - Refresh/renew credentials

## Success Criteria

- [ ] JWT expiration checked for all OAuth-based providers
- [ ] API key validity checked where possible
- [ ] Warning thresholds configurable
- [ ] Clear remediation suggestions provided
- [ ] Doctor output includes credential health
- [ ] Proactive warnings in usage output

## Dependencies

- **Extends**: Existing doctor module infrastructure
- **Independent of**: Other EPICs (can be built standalone)
- **Existing code**: JWT decoding already exists in codex provider

## Considerations

- Not all auth methods have checkable expiration (API keys)
- Some providers don't expose expiration info
- Avoid excessive validation requests (rate limits)
- Cache validation results appropriately","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:12:27.100820643Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T18:12:27.100820643Z","compaction_level":0}

- `caut auth status` - Credential status for all providers
- `caut auth refresh <provider>` - Refresh/renew credentials

## Success Criteria

- [ ] JWT expiration checked for all OAuth-based providers

- [ ] Warning thresholds configurable
- [ ] Clear remediation suggestions provided
- [ ] Doctor output includes credential health
- [ ] Proactive warnings in usage output

## Dependencies

- **Extends**: Existing doctor module infrastructure
- **Independent of**: Other EPICs (can be built standalone)
- **Existing code**: JWT decoding already exists in codex provider

## Considerations

- Not all auth methods have checkable expiration (API keys)
- Some providers don't expose expiration info
- Avoid excessive validation requests (rate limits)
- Cache validation results appropriately","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:12:27.100820643Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:58:35.406314493Z","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-32d","title":"Create test data fixtures and factories","description":"## Overview
Build reusable test data fixtures for consistent, realistic test scenarios.

## Requirements
- Provider response fixtures (Claude, Codex) - **NOT Cursor (provider does not exist)**
- Rate window test data at various percentages
- Status page response samples
- Cost calculation test data
- Error response fixtures
- Token account fixtures
- Cost scanner cache fixtures

## Implementation Details
- Create `tests/fixtures/` directory structure
- JSON fixture files for provider responses
- Rust factory functions in `tests/common/fixtures.rs`
- Builder patterns for complex test objects

## Claude Provider Fixtures (CRITICAL for 8gp)
1. **credentials.json samples**
   ```json
   // tests/fixtures/claude/credentials_full.json
   {"account_email": "user@example.com", "organization": "Anthropic"}
   // tests/fixtures/claude/credentials_minimal.json
   {"account_email": "user@example.com"}
   // tests/fixtures/claude/credentials_empty.json
   {}
   ```

2. **CLI limits output samples**
   ```text
   // tests/fixtures/claude/cli_output_v0.2.105.txt
   Session limit: 70% remaining
   Weekly limit: 85% remaining
   Opus/Sonnet: 92% remaining
   // tests/fixtures/claude/cli_output_legacy.txt
   Rate limit: 45% remaining (resets in 2h)
   ```

3. **ClaudeRateLimitResponse JSON**
   ```json
   // tests/fixtures/claude/rate_limit_full.json
   {"rate_limit": {"remaining_percent": 70, "resets_at": "..."}, "credits": {...}}
   // tests/fixtures/claude/rate_limit_partial.json
   // tests/fixtures/claude/rate_limit_empty.json
   ```

## Codex Provider Fixtures (CRITICAL for 2k1)
1. **auth.json with JWT**
   ```json
   // tests/fixtures/codex/auth_oauth.json
   {
     "tokens": {
       "id_token": "<base64-jwt-with-openai-claims>",
       "access_token": "...",
       "account_id": "acct_xxx"
     }
   }
   // tests/fixtures/codex/auth_api_key.json
   {"OPENAI_API_KEY": "sk-xxx"}
   // tests/fixtures/codex/auth_both.json
   ```

2. **Sample JWT payload (sanitized)**
   ```json
   // tests/fixtures/codex/jwt_claims_sample.json
   {
     "email": "user@example.com",
     "email_verified": true,
     "https://api.openai.com/auth": {
       "chatgpt_plan_type": "pro",
       "chatgpt_subscription_active_until": "2026-02-01",
       "organizations": [{"title": "Personal", "is_default": true}]
     }
   }
   ```

3. **CodexRateLimitResponse variants**

   // tests/fixtures/codex/rate_limit_full.json
   // tests/fixtures/codex/rate_limit_credits_only.json
   // tests/fixtures/codex/rate_limit_user_only.json

- [ ] Rust factory functions implemented
- [ ] Fixture loading utility

 {"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-36t","title":"[EPIC] Graphical TUI Dashboard for Watch Mode","description":"## Overview

Transform the existing watch mode into a rich, real-time terminal dashboard with charts, sparklines, and visual status indicators using **ratatui** (the actively maintained TUI library for Rust).

## Strategic Importance (Why P2)

- **Visual appeal matters**: For adoption and demos
- **Enhances existing feature**: Watch mode already exists, this makes it great
- **Proven ecosystem**: ratatui is the standard Rust TUI library
- **Differentiates**: Most CLI tools have boring output

## User Value Proposition

**Current watch mode**: Basic text refresh, functional but uninspiring.

**Target state**:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  caut watch                                    Updated: 12:34:56 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│  CLAUDE                           CODEX                          │
│  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ │
│  Primary:  ████████░░ 78%         Primary:  ████░░░░░░ 42%       │
│  Weekly:   ██████░░░░ 61%         Credits:  $87.50 remaining     │
│                                                                  │
│  Trend (1h): ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█ ↗ +23%     Trend (1h): ▃▃▄▄▄▅▅▅ → stable  │
│  ETA limit: ~45m ⚠️               ETA limit: sustainable ✓       │
│                                                                  │
│  Cost today: $12.34               Cost today: $3.21              │
│  Cost MTD:   $156.78              Cost MTD:   $45.67             │
│                                                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [q] quit  [r] refresh  [p] pause  [1-4] focus provider         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Technical Approach

### Dependencies
```toml
[dependencies]
ratatui = "0.26"
crossterm = "0.27"
```

### Architecture
```rust
use ratatui::{
    backend::CrosstermBackend,
    layout::{Constraint, Direction, Layout},
    widgets::{Block, Borders, Gauge, Sparkline, Paragraph},
    Terminal,
};

struct DashboardApp {
    providers: Vec<ProviderState>,
    history: HashMap<String, Vec<f64>>,
    focused_panel: usize,
    paused: bool,
}

impl DashboardApp {
    fn render(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let chunks = Layout::default()
            .direction(Direction::Vertical)
            .constraints([
                Constraint::Length(3),  // Header
                Constraint::Min(0),     // Main content
                Constraint::Length(3),  // Footer
            ])
            .split(frame.size());
        
        self.render_header(frame, chunks[0]);
        self.render_providers(frame, chunks[1]);
        self.render_footer(frame, chunks[2]);
    }
}
```

### Sparkline Implementation
```rust
fn render_sparkline(history: &[f64]) -> Sparkline {
    let data: Vec<u64> = history.iter()
        .map(|v| (v * 100.0) as u64)
        .collect();
    
    Sparkline::default()
        .data(&data)
        .style(Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan))
}
```

### Keyboard Interactions
- `q` - Quit
- `r` - Force refresh
- `p` - Pause/resume updates
- `1-9` - Focus specific provider
- `h` - Toggle help panel
- `j/k` or `↑/↓` - Navigate providers

## Layout Modes

1. **Dashboard** (default): All providers in grid
2. **Focus**: Single provider detailed view
3. **Compact**: Minimal, single-line per provider

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Rich visual dashboard renders correctly
- [ ] Sparklines show recent trend from history
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works smoothly
- [ ] Responsive layout adapts to terminal size
- [ ] Color themes work (and can be disabled with NO_COLOR)
- [ ] Performance: Smooth updates at 1-second intervals
- [ ] Accessibility: Works without colors, high contrast mode

## Dependencies

- **REQUIRES**: Historical Usage Tracking (EPIC 1) - for sparklines
- **Uses**: ratatui, crossterm

## Considerations

- Terminal compatibility: Test across terminals (iTerm2, Windows Terminal, Linux)
- SSH performance: Don't assume fast connection
- Color blindness: Ensure info conveyed without color alone
